calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Feb-15-2006 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: 9:30am
Judge Pro Tem Peter Hadiaris, clerk-Beth Wilber, nothing on record:
Cause called for Petition on rehearing. Julie A. Darbellay, appears as respondent in Pro Per, Dennis Michael Carr, Esq. appearing for appellant Mei Wong. Ms. Darbellay and Ms. Wong are sworn as witnesses. (Judge Hadiaris gives an oral review of what happened at the previous 1/5/06 hearing, that Ms. Darbellay was not present for. The Court then hears testimony related to the request for a rehearing from parties.
Ms. Barbara Watson, an insurance claims representative, previously sworn, testifies in the narrative in response to questions from the court.
9:45am
Respondent's petition for a rehearing is granted and Judge Hadiaris calls the small claims appeal. Ms. Darbellay, as respondent, testifies in the narrative. Cross-examination by Mr. Carr, redirect by Ms. Darbellay. No further questions, respondent rests.
10:06am
Mr. Carr begins direct examination of Ms. Wong.
10:16am
Cross-examination by Ms. Darbellay, redirect by Mr. Carr. No further questions, appellant rests.
10:25am
The matter is submitted. The court rules in favor of the appellant, Mei Wong. Evidence was retained by the court.

Judge Hadiaris left before signing the judgment form or Pro Tem stipulation. They will have to be sent to him for his for his signature, and returned to the court, before a judgment can be entered.

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jan-05-2006 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: 10:00am
Judge Pro Tem Hadiaris, clerk-beth wilber, nothing on record:
Cause called for small claims appeal. Dennis M.Carr, Esq. appears for appellant Mei Wong. There was no appearance by Julie A. Darbellay, Pro Per respondent. Mr. Carr informs Judge Hadiaris that Ms. Wong had filed a cross-complaint against the respondent. Mr. Carr begins direct examination of Mei Wong, previously sworn. Mr. Carr gives closing remarks for the appellant, and rests. The court reviews but does not identify or retain any new evidence.
10:35am
court adjourns.
